seminal_paper_arxiv: Mazur, B. — Rational isogenies of prime degree, Inventiones Mathematicae 44 (1978), 129-162
bridge_lemma: BHV 2001 (Crelle 539, 75-122) gives n>30 implies U_n has a primitive prime divisor, so the k=3 stratum n>30 with 3|n is the trivial corollary. The Mazur-torsion auxiliary lever proposes (suspectly) that no primitive divisor of U_n forces reducibility of rho_{E,p} on E_{R,Q}: y^2=x(x^2+Rx+Q) for every p|U_n, hence p in Mazur 1978 Inventiones 44 list {2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19,37,43,67,163}, contradicting cyclotomic density. Codex GPT-5-5 flags this bridge generally invalid since ell|U_n encodes order in a residue torus, not a Galois-stable line in E[ell].
informal_proof_outline: Step 1 (Trivial corollary path). Bilu-Hanrot-Voutier 2001 (J. Reine Angew. Math. 539, 75-122) prove: for every Lehmer pair (alpha,beta) and every n > 30, U_n has a primitive prime divisor. The k=3 stratum (n > 30 and 3 divides n) is the trivial corollary because n > 30 and 3 divides n implies n > 30. This is the direct route; no Mazur transplant needed. Step 2 (Companion-curve formalization). Associate the elliptic curve E_{R,Q}/Q: y^2 = x(x^2 + R x + Q), with discriminant Delta_E = 16 Q^2 (R^2 - 4 Q). For primes ell not dividing 2 Q (R^2 - 4 Q), the criterion ell divides U_n is equivalent to the order of alpha/beta in (F_ell-bar)^x divides n; primitive means the order equals n. This is the Stewart 1977-Voutier 1995-BHV 2001 framework, NOT a Frey-Mazur statement about Galois-stable lines in E[ell]. Step 3 (Mazur-torsion auxiliary lever). Mazur 1978 Inventiones 44 Theorem 1: any rational p-isogeny on an elliptic curve E/Q has prime degree p in {2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, 19, 37, 43, 67, 163} (the last three are CM). Reducibility of rho_{E,p} on E_{R,Q} is equivalent to a rational p-isogeny. Step 4 (Suspect bridge - explicit warning). The proposed transplant claims: if U_n has no primitive divisor then rho_{E,p} is reducible for every p|U_n, forcing p in Mazur's 12-element list, and a cyclotomic-density argument then produces a p|U_n outside the list. Codex GPT-5.5 verification (May 30 2026) flags Step 4 as generally invalid: ell divides U_n encodes the order of alpha/beta in a residue torus, not the existence of a Galois-stable line in E[ell]; the precise Frey-Mazur reduction is missing in Stewart-Voutier-BHV. The composite cyclic isogeny list (Kenku-Mazur) gives n in {1,..,19,21,25,27,37,43,67,163} for rational cyclic n-isogenies; with 3 divides n that is n in {3,6,9,12,15,18,21,27}. X_0(15) and X_0(21) admit noncuspidal rational points. Step 5 (Honest submission framing). Submit the trivial-corollary path as the main claim and the Mazur transplant as a falsification probe: invite Aristotle to (a) confirm primitive_divisor_k3_trivial via bhv_primitive_divisor_gt_30_axiom, (b) attempt to repair frey_mazur_bridge_suspect via a Frey-Mazur-style auxiliary construction, or (c) reject the Mazur lever entirely. The submission documents the W2-D1 transplant attempt and its feasibility-filter outcome.
candidate_lemmas: lehmer_companion_curve: for any Lehmer pair (alpha,beta) with alpha+beta = sqrt(R), alpha*beta = Q, the elliptic curve E_{R,Q}: y^2 = x(x^2 + R x + Q) over Q has discriminant 16 Q^2 (R^2 - 4 Q) and is the standard Lucas-companion curve attached to (R,Q).

lehmer_prime_order_criterion: for any prime ell not dividing 2 Q (R^2 - 4 Q), ell divides U_n if and only if the order of alpha/beta in the residue torus (F_ell-bar)^x divides n; primitivity is exactness of this order.

bhv_primitive_divisor_gt_30_axiom: Bilu-Hanrot-Voutier 2001 J. Reine Angew. Math. 539 75-122 theorem stated as an auxiliary axiom: for every Lehmer pair (alpha,beta) and every n > 30, U_n has a primitive prime divisor (Mathlib does not yet have BHV formalized).

mazur_prime_isogeny_degree_axiom: Mazur 1978 Inventiones 44 129-162 stated as an auxiliary axiom: any rational p-isogeny on an elliptic curve E/Q has prime degree p in the 12-element set {2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, 19, 37, 43, 67, 163} (the last three are CM cases).

primitive_divisor_k3_trivial: for every Lehmer pair (alpha,beta) and every n > 30 with 3 divides n, U_n has a primitive prime divisor (immediate corollary of bhv_primitive_divisor_gt_30_axiom: n > 30 and 3 divides n implies n > 30).

frey_mazur_bridge_suspect: SUSPECT/EXPLORATORY auxiliary statement under feasibility-filter warning: if U_n has no primitive prime divisor, then for every prime p dividing U_n the mod-p Galois representation rho_{E,p} on E_{R,Q}-torsion is reducible. Codex GPT-5.5 verification flags this as generally invalid; submitted as falsification probe.
honest_disclaimer: YOLO-W2-D1, wave-2 angle B for Erdos 1052 / Wall k=3 Lehmer primitive divisor. Wave-1 (YOLO4 slot 1291) used Borel-Tits-Voskresenskii torus-rank in algebraic groups; this submission uses MODULAR GALOIS REPRESENTATIONS (Mazur torsion). FEASIBILITY-FILTER WARNING (Codex GPT-5.5 verification May 30 2026): the load-bearing bridge claim no_primitive_divisor_implies_reducible_mod_p is GENERALLY INVALID. ell | U_n says alpha/beta has order dividing n in a residue torus, NOT that there is a Galois-stable line in E[ell]. Reducibility of rho_{E,ell} is equivalent to a rational ell-isogeny on E_{R,Q} but does NOT follow from ell | U_n. Mazur's complete prime-degree list is {2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19,37,43,67,163} (last three CM); X_0(15) and X_0(21) DO have noncuspidal rational points. BHV at k=3 follows trivially: n>30 and 3|n implies n>30 so BHV applies directly; Mazur is not load-bearing. Submission is therefore filed as a FALSIFICATION/exploration: Aristotle is invited to either (a) repair the bridge via a Frey-Mazur-style auxiliary construction, (b) confirm BHV at k=3 via direct citation as the trivial corollary, or (c) reject the Mazur-transplant lever. Plausibility of full closure: ~0.05. Plausibility of meaningful sub-claim (companion-curve construction + BHV citation): ~0.30. Mathlib does NOT have BHV, Mazur, or X_0(N); Aristotle would need auxiliary axioms (bhv_primitive_divisor_gt_30, mazur_prime_isogeny_degree_axiom). Prior submissions (slot306-355, slot567, slot623, slot692, slot1265, slot1282) targeted unitary-perfect; slot 1291 was first BHV-interpretation (torus-rank angle); this is the SECOND BHV-interpretation submission (Mazur-Galois angle). FUSION + informal-reasoner is the vehicle for an honest 'try this even though the bridge is suspect' submission per W8.
